name: preoccupied-koji-typing

metadata.name: preoccupied.koji-typing
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: types and annotations for the koji build system

documentation:
# Overview

`preoccupied.koji-typing` is an experimental distribution which
provides typing support for the [koji] package.

It is currently a Work In-Progress, there are still many signatures
to be added. The initial starting point was simply the set of
hub calls that were being used by [koji-smoky-dingo]. This project
aims to fill out everything.

[koji-smoky-dingo]: https://github.com/obriencj/koji-smoky-dingo

[koji]: https://pagure.io/koji

**This project is neither enodorsed, nor supported, by the upstream
[Koji] project**.

This repository exists because I had a number of opinions on how to
produce the typing support. There is an open [issue] in the upstream
to start including typing stubs. However, because koji still supports
Python 2, they are limited to only providing stubs. This means that
their `TypedDict` declarations are also constrained purely to the stub
definitions. The problem with this approach is that one cannot import
typing declarations from a stub for use in a non-stub. While [MyPy]
can infer some of the fields from the usage of the return values this
way, anything more complex than mutating the fields in the same
function they are obtained becomes un-checkable. One could not declare
helper functions and then annotate them to make it clear that they
operate specifically on the `BuildInfo` structure, because `BuildInfo`
could not be imported and used for the annotation during runtime
loading of the hypothetical module.

[issue]: https://pagure.io/koji/issue/3708


## Runtime package

The runtime-available `koji_types` package provides a number of
`TypedDict` definitions which provide structure for the numerous
dictionary result types returned by koji's `ClientSession`
interface. These types can be used to annotate your client code in
order to later perform anaylsis. It also provides some Pythonic
enumerations for some koji constant values.


## Static analysis package

Following [PEP-561] guidelines the packages `koij-stubs`,
`koji_cli-stubs`, and `kojihub-stubs` provide partial stub annotations
for use during static analysis with tools like [MyPy]. These all rely
on the `koji_types` package definitions in order to supply accurate
signatures for many of the dict-based results.

[PEP-561]: https://peps.python.org/pep-0561/

[MyPy]: https://mypy-lang.org


## Caveats

I have not been able to figure out how to provide typing annotations
to correctly reflect the return type change of `ClientSession` calls
which support a `queryOpts` in the use case of `countOnly = True`. In
those calls the return type is actually an `int` but I haven't found a
way to provide an override annotation that shows this.

```python
# here the return type is List[UserInfo]
friends = session.listUsers()

# here the return type is actually int, but no annotation support
# exists for this scenario so static analysis will still think
# it's a List[UserInfo]
howmany = session.listUsers(queryOpts={"countOnly": True})
```


## Contact

Author: Christopher O'Brien  <obriencj@preoccupied.net>

Original Git Repository: <https://github.com/obriencj/koji-typing>

modules.name: koji_types.protocols
documentation:
Koji Types - Client Session Protocols

The majority of definitions are in the typing stub for this module,
where they can be used for static analysis without impacting
runtime. During analysis these definitions will become Protocols, with
all of the various methods listed. However, we don't want the abstract
definitions of a Protocol to prevent subclass instantiation, so at
runtime these are just empty classes.

:author: Christopher O'Brien  <obriencj@gmail.com>

name identifying the repo that this RPM came from, or 'INTERNAL'

headers)

Data representing a koji RPM. These are typically obtained via the

Data representing an RPM signature in koji. Obtained via the

documentation: space-separated list of architectures this host can handle

documentation:
maximum capacity for tasks, using the sum of the task weight
values

class_variables.name: koji_types.HostInfo.comment
documentation: text describing the current status or usage

class_variables.name: koji_types.HostInfo.description
documentation: text describing this host

class_variables.name: koji_types.HostInfo.enabled
documentation: whether this host is configured by the hub to take tasks

class_variables.name: koji_types.HostInfo.id
documentation: internal identifier

class_variables.name: koji_types.HostInfo.name
documentation: user name of this host's account, normally FQDN.

class_variables.name: koji_types.HostInfo.ready
documentation:
whether this host is reporting itself as active and prepared to
accept tasks

class_variables.name: koji_types.HostInfo.task_load
documentation:
the load of currently running tasks on the host. Compared with the
capacity and a given task's weight, this can determine whether a
task will 'fit' on the host

class_variables.name: koji_types.HostInfo.user_id
documentation:
the user ID of this host's account. Hosts have a user account of
type HOST, which is how they authenticate with the hub

Data representing a koji host. These are typically obtained via the
`getHost` XMLRPC call

class_variables.name: koji_types.RepoInfo.create_event
documentation:
koji event ID representing the point that the repo's tag
configuration was snapshot from. Note that this doesn't always
correlate to the creation time of the repo -- koji has the ability to
generate a repository based on older events

class_variables.name: koji_types.RepoInfo.create_ts
documentation: UTC timestamp indicating when this repo was created

class_variables.name: koji_types.RepoInfo.creation_time
documentation:
ISO-8601 formatted UTC datetime stamp indicating when this repo
was created

documentation: whether this is a dist-repo or not

class_variables.name: koji_types.RepoInfo.id
documentation: internal ID for this repository

class_variables.name: koji_types.RepoInfo.state
documentation: the current state of this repository

class_variables.name: koji_types.RepoInfo.tag_id
documentation:
ID of the tag from which this repo was generated. This value is not
present in the output of the `getRepo` XMLRPC call as it is presumed
that the caller already knows the tag's identity

class_variables.name: koji_types.RepoInfo.tag_name
documentation:
name of the tag from which this repo was generated. This value is
not present in the output of the `getRepo` XMLRPC call as it is
presumed that the caller already knows the tag's identity

class_variables.name: koji_types.RepoInfo.task_id
documentation: ID of the task which generated this repository

Data representing a koji build tag's repository. These are
typically obtained via the `getRepo` or `repoInfo` XMLRPC
calls

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagInfo.maven_include_all
documentation:
whether this tag should use the alternative maven-latest logic
(including multiple builds of the same package name) when inherited
by the build tag of a maven-enabled target

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagInfo.maven_support
documentation:
whether this tag should generate a maven repository when it is
the build tag for a target

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagInfo.perm
documentation:
name of the required permission to associate builds with this tag,
or None

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagInfo.perm_id
documentation:
ID of the required permission to associate builds with this tag,
or None

Data representing a koji tag. Typically obtained via the
`getTag` XMLRPC call

classes.name: koji_types.TagInheritanceEntry

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagInheritanceEntry.child_id
documentation:
the ID of the child tag in the inheritance link. The child tag
inherits from the parent tag

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagInheritanceEntry.intransitive
documentation:
if true then this inheritance link would not be inherited. ie.
this link only appears at a depth of 1, and is otherwise
omitted.

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagInheritanceEntry.maxdepth
documentation:
additional parents in the inheritance tree from this link are
only considered up to this depth, relative from the link's current
depth.  A maxdepth of 1 indicates that only the immediate parents
will be inherited. A maxdepth of 0 indicates that the tag and none
of its parents will be inherited. A value of None indicates no
restriction.

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagInheritanceEntry.name
documentation: the parent tag's name

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagInheritanceEntry.noconfig
documentation:
if True then this inheritance link does not include tag
configuration data, such as extras and groups

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagInheritanceEntry.parent_id
documentation: the parent tag's internal ID

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagInheritanceEntry.pkg_filter
documentation:
a regex indicating which package entries may be inherited. If
empty, all packages are inherited

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagInheritanceEntry.priority
documentation:
the inheritance link priority, which provides an ordering for
links at the same depth with the same child tag (ie. what order
the parent links for a given tag are processed in). Lower
priorities are processed first.

Data representing a single inheritance element. A list of these
represents the inheritance data for a tag. Typically obtained via
the `getInheritanceData` XMLRPC call.

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagFullInheritanceEntry.currdepth
documentation:
only present from the `getFullInheritance` call. The
inheritance depth this link occurs at. A depth of 1 indicates that
the child tag would be the one originally queried for its
inheritance tree

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagFullInheritanceEntry.filter
documentation: only present from the `getFullInheritance` call.

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagFullInheritanceEntry.nextdepth
documentation: only present from the `getFullInheritance` call.

class_variables.name: koji_types.PackageInfo.name
documentation: the package name

classes.name: koji_types.TagPackageSimple

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagPackageSimple.package_name
documentation: name of the package
``listPackagesSimple`` XMLRPC call.

classes.name: koji_types.TagPackageInfo

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagPackageInfo.blocked
documentation: if True this entry represents a block

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagPackageInfo.extra_arches
documentation: additional architectures, separated by spaces

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagPackageInfo.owner_id
documentation: ID of the user who is the owner of the package for this tag

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagPackageInfo.owner_name
documentation: name of the user who is the owner of the package for this tag

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagPackageInfo.tag_id
documentation: ID of the package listing's tag

class_variables.name: koji_types.TagPackageInfo.tag_name
documentation: name of the package listing's tag
``listPackages`` XMLRPC call.

classes.name: koji_types.TagGroupInfo
documentation: ``getTagGroups`` XMLRPC call

classes.name: koji_types.TaskInfo

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.arch
documentation: task architecture, or 'noarch'

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.awaited
documentation:
True if this task is currently being waiting-for by its parent
task.  False if this task is no longer being waited-for. None if
the task was never waited-for.

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.channel_id
documentation:
internal ID of the channel from which a host will be selected to
take this task

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.completion_time
documentation:
ISO-8601 formatted UTC datetime stamp indicating when this task
was completed, or None if not completed

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.completion_ts
documentation:
UTC timestamp indicating when this task was completed, or None if
not completed

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.create_time
documentation:
ISO-8601 formatted UTC datetime stamp indicating when this task
was created

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.create_ts
documentation: UTC timestamp indicating when this task was created

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.host_id
documentation: host which has taken this task, or None

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.id
documentation: internal task ID

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.label
documentation: task label, or None

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.method
documentation: task method, indicates the type of work to be done

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.owner
documentation: ID of the user that initiated this task

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.parent
documentation: ID of the parent task, or None

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.start_time
documentation:
ISO-8601 formatted UTC datetime stamp indicating when this task
was started by a host, or None if not yet started

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.start_ts
documentation:
UTC timestamp indicating when this task was started by a host, or
None if not yet started

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.state
documentation: the current state of this task

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.waiting
documentation:
True if this task is currently waiting for any of its subtasks to
complete. False if this task is not waiting, or None if the task
never needed to wait.

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.weight
documentation:
value which ascribes the general resources needed to perform this
task. hosts have a limit to the number of resources which can be used
to run tasks in parallel

class_variables.name: koji_types.TaskInfo.request
documentation:
The task request info. Only present when the request parameter to
the `getTaskInfo` call is `True`. Note that the `as_taskinfo`
function does set that parameter to True.
``getTaskInfo`` XMLRPC call

class_variables.name: koji_types.ChannelInfo.id
documentation: internal channel ID

class_variables.name: koji_types.ChannelInfo.name
documentation: channel name

classes.name: koji_types.SearchResult

class_variables.name: koji_types.SearchResult.id
documentation: result ID

class_variables.name: koji_types.SearchResult.name
documentation: result name
as returned by the `search` XMLRPC call

classes.name: koji_types.GOptions
documentation:
Represents the koji client configuration options as provided by the
baseline koji CLI.

`Values` instances with these fields are fed to a `CLIHandler`

Returned by the `get_options` function from within the koji CLI
utility, which cannot be imported normally. Default values for
these are pulled from the profile configuration if unspecified as
base CLI arguments.

classes.name: koji_types.QueryOptions
documentation:
Various API calls use queryOpts dictionary for altering output
format

classes.name: koji_types.FilterOptions
documentation:
the `filterOpts` argument for for `filterResults` and
countAndFilterResults\` XMLRPC calls

classes.name: koji_types.ListTasksOptions
documentation: Specific filter dictionary for listTasks API call

class_variables.name: koji_types.RPMFileInfo.rpm_id
